gomodel:run-figure1-008/tph1/enabled_by.snippet: Symbol tph-1 resolved to stable WormBase gene ID WB:WBGene00006600 (C. elegans).

gomodel:run-figure1-008/ser6/enabled_by.snippet: Symbol ser-6 resolved to stable WormBase gene ID WB:WBGene00021897 (C. elegans).

gomodel:run-figure1-008/mod1/enabled_by.snippet: Symbol mod-1 resolved to stable WormBase gene ID WB:WBGene00003386 (C. elegans).

gomodel:run-figure1-008/tbh1/enabled_by.snippet: Symbol tbh-1 resolved to stable WormBase gene ID WB:WBGene00006541 (C. elegans).

gomodel:run-figure1-008/nhr76/enabled_by.snippet: Symbol nhr-76 resolved to stable WormBase gene ID WB:WBGene00015497 (C. elegans).

gomodel:run-figure1-008/atgl1/enabled_by.snippet: Figure node 'ATGL' resolved to C. elegans adipose triglyceride lipase atgl-1, stable WormBase gene ID WB:WBGene00015484.

gomodel:run-figure1-008/tph1/molecular_f.snippet: tph-1 is annotated to tryptophan 5-monooxygenase activity (rate-limiting enzyme of serotonin biosynthesis).

gomodel:run-figure1-008/tph1/part_of.snippet: tph-1 is annotated to serotonin biosynthetic process.

gomodel:run-figure1-008/tph1/occurs_in/c.snippet: Blue triangle labeled 'ADF' inside the NEURONS oval contains tph-1; activity placed in the ADF serotonergic neuron.

gomodel:run-figure1-008/tph1/occurs_in.snippet: tph-1 is annotated to neuron projection.

gomodel:run-figure1-008/tph1/has_output/.snippet: tph-1 (tryptophan hydroxylase) synthesizes serotonin (5-HT, CHEBI:28790); the amine is the enzymatic product and the relay signal to the URX mod-1 channel (Panel E arrow labeled 5-HT).

gomodel:run-figure1-008/ser6/molecular_f.snippet: ser-6 is annotated to octopamine receptor activity (IDA).

gomodel:run-figure1-008/ser6/part_of.snippet: ser-6 is annotated to octopamine signaling pathway (IDA).

gomodel:run-figure1-008/ser6/occurs_in/c.snippet: Green triangle labeled 'AWB' inside the NEURONS oval contains ser-6; activity placed in the AWB neuron.

gomodel:run-figure1-008/ser6/occurs_in.snippet: ser-6 is annotated to plasma membrane (IBA); the GPCR sits in the plasma membrane.

gomodel:run-figure1-008/ser6/has_small_m.snippet: Octopamine (CHEBI:17134) is the direct ligand that activates the SER-6 octopamine receptor; Panel E arrow labeled 'Oct' from RIC (tbh-1) to AWB (ser-6).

gomodel:run-figure1-008/mod1/molecular_f.snippet: mod-1 is annotated to serotonin-gated chloride channel activity (IDA).

gomodel:run-figure1-008/mod1/part_of.snippet: mod-1 is annotated to serotonin receptor signaling pathway (IDA).

gomodel:run-figure1-008/mod1/occurs_in/c.snippet: Yellow hexagon labeled 'URX' inside the NEURONS oval contains mod-1; activity placed in the URX neuron.

gomodel:run-figure1-008/mod1/occurs_in.snippet: mod-1 is annotated to plasma membrane (IDA); the serotonin-gated channel sits in the plasma membrane.

gomodel:run-figure1-008/mod1/has_small_m.snippet: Serotonin (5-HT, CHEBI:28790) is the direct ligand that gates/activates the MOD-1 serotonin-gated chloride channel; same molecule produced by tph-1 in ADF (Panel E arrow labeled 5-HT).

gomodel:run-figure1-008/tbh1/molecular_f.snippet: tbh-1 is annotated to tyramine-beta hydroxylase activity (IMP); enzyme converting tyramine to octopamine.

gomodel:run-figure1-008/tbh1/part_of.snippet: tbh-1 is annotated to octopamine biosynthetic process (IMP).

gomodel:run-figure1-008/tbh1/occurs_in/c.snippet: Red pentagon labeled 'RIC' inside the NEURONS oval contains tbh-1; activity placed in the RIC octopaminergic neuron.

gomodel:run-figure1-008/tbh1/has_output/.snippet: tbh-1 (tyramine beta-hydroxylase) synthesizes octopamine (CHEBI:17134); the amine is the enzymatic product and the relay signal that activates the AWB ser-6 receptor (Panel E arrow labeled Oct).

gomodel:run-figure1-008/nhr76/molecular_.snippet: nhr-76 is annotated to nuclear receptor activity (ligand-dependent DNA-binding transcription factor).

gomodel:run-figure1-008/nhr76/part_of.snippet: nhr-76 is annotated to positive regulation of transcription by RNA polymerase II (IMP); in this circuit nhr-76 activates expression of the lipase atgl-1.
gomodel:run-figure1-008/nhr76/part_of.term_label: positive regulation of transcription by RNA polymerase II

gomodel:run-figure1-008/nhr76/occurs_in/.snippet: nhr-76 is the first node of the intestinal sub-pathway in the INTESTINE box (Panel E); activity placed in the intestine.

gomodel:run-figure1-008/nhr76/occurs_in.snippet: nhr-76 is annotated to nucleus (IBA); the nuclear receptor acts in the nucleus.

gomodel:run-figure1-008/nhr76/has_input/.snippet: nhr-76 transcriptionally activates the intestinal lipase atgl-1 (ATGL), its transcriptional target gene (Panel E arrow nhr-76 -> ATGL).

gomodel:run-figure1-008/atgl1/molecular_.snippet: atgl-1 (ATGL) is annotated to triacylglycerol lipase activity; hydrolyzes triglycerides.

gomodel:run-figure1-008/atgl1/part_of.snippet: atgl-1 is annotated to triglyceride catabolic process (lipolysis); captures the figure's ATGL -> beta-oxidation / Fat loss outcome arrow (a downstream process, not a gene).

gomodel:run-figure1-008/atgl1/occurs_in/.snippet: ATGL is the second node of the intestinal sub-pathway in the INTESTINE box (Panel E); activity placed in the intestine.

gomodel:run-figure1-008/atgl1/occurs_in.snippet: atgl-1 is annotated to lipid droplet (IDA); the lipase acts at the lipid droplet surface.

gomodel:run-figure1-008/tph1/causal/gomo.snippet: tph-1 (ADF) serotonin biosynthesis is causally upstream, positive effect, of the URX mod-1 serotonin-gated channel, via the diffusible intermediate 5-HT (serotonin, CHEBI:28790; produced by tph-1, gates mod-1). Panel E solid arrow ADF->URX labeled '5-HT'.

gomodel:run-figure1-008/tbh1/causal/gomo.snippet: tbh-1 (RIC) octopamine biosynthesis is causally upstream, positive effect, of the AWB ser-6 octopamine receptor, via the diffusible intermediate octopamine (CHEBI:17134; produced by tbh-1, activates ser-6). Panel E solid arrow RIC->AWB labeled 'Oct'.

gomodel:run-figure1-008/ser6/causal/gomo.snippet: Panel E shows a solid pointed arrow from AWB (ser-6) to ADF (tph-1) on the left of the NEURONS oval, read as ser-6 positively regulating tph-1. LOW CONFIDENCE (0.3) - flagged unconfirmed against the figure during verification; no specific citation located for this exact ser-6 -> tph-1 step. Curator should verify directionality/existence.

gomodel:run-figure1-008/mod1/causal/gomo.snippet: mod-1 (URX) indirectly positively regulates tbh-1 (RIC) octopamine biosynthesis. Panel E dashed arrow URX->RIC indicates an indirect/modulatory step within the neuronal relay.

The URX neuron (mod-1) releases an endocrine signal that reaches the intestine and activates nhr-76. Compartment-level edge NEURONS(URX)->INTESTINE modeled as mod-1 activity indirectly positively regulating intestinal nhr-76 activity, via a secreted endocrine signal (Panel E dashed arrow labeled 'endocrine signal'; black-dot vesicle clusters near URX). Signal identity unspecified - indirect relation reflects the unidentified intervening endocrine messenger.

gomodel:run-figure1-008/nhr76/causal/gom.snippet: nhr-76 (intestinal nuclear receptor/TF) indirectly positively regulates atgl-1 (ATGL) lipase activity by activating its transcription (gene expression is the intervening module). Panel E solid arrow nhr-76 -> ATGL within the INTESTINE box.
